Test Scenario Reference

Complete reference of all 3,553 test scenarios across TLS, HTTP/2, QUIC, and Raw TCP protocols. Each scenario is a self-contained test case with a defined expected outcome.

1964

234

A — Handshake Order Violations (Client)

TLS HIGH 10 scenarios

Tests whether the target correctly rejects TLS handshake messages received in illegal order. A properly implemented TLS state machine should send a fatal alert and terminate the connection when it receives a Finished, ClientKeyExchange, or Certificate message before a ClientHello.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 out-of-order-finished-first-small-ch Send Finished before ClientHello [small CH] client DROPPED
2 out-of-order-finished-first-pqc-ch Send Finished before ClientHello [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 out-of-order-cke-before-hello-small-ch Send ClientKeyExchange before ClientHello [small CH] client DROPPED
4 out-of-order-cke-before-hello-pqc-ch Send ClientKeyExchange before ClientHello [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 duplicate-client-hello-small-ch Send ClientHello twice [small CH] client DROPPED
6 duplicate-client-hello-pqc-ch Send ClientHello twice [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 client-hello-after-finished-small-ch Send ClientHello, receive ServerHello, then send another ClientHello [small CH] client DROPPED
8 client-hello-after-finished-pqc-ch Send ClientHello, receive ServerHello, then send another ClientHello [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 skip-client-key-exchange-small-ch ClientHello then jump straight to ChangeCipherSpec + Finished [small CH] client DROPPED
10 skip-client-key-exchange-pqc-ch ClientHello then jump straight to ChangeCipherSpec + Finished [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

C — Parameter Mutation

TLS MEDIUM 8 scenarios

Mutates fields within an otherwise valid ClientHello to test parser robustness. Includes version downgrades (advertising TLS 1.0 in a 1.2 handshake), session ID corruption, SNI hostname mismatches, and random value overwrites.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 version-downgrade-mid-handshake-small-ch ClientHello says TLS 1.2, then CKE record header says TLS 1.0 [small CH] client DROPPED
2 version-downgrade-mid-handshake-pqc-ch ClientHello says TLS 1.2, then CKE record header says TLS 1.0 [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 session-id-mutation-small-ch Change session ID between handshake messages [small CH] client DROPPED
4 session-id-mutation-pqc-ch Change session ID between handshake messages [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 sni-mismatch-small-ch Send ClientHello with SNI "a.com", then another with SNI "b.com" [small CH] client DROPPED
6 sni-mismatch-pqc-ch Send ClientHello with SNI "a.com", then another with SNI "b.com" [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 random-overwrite-small-ch Send identical ClientHello but with different random value [small CH] client DROPPED
8 random-overwrite-pqc-ch Send identical ClientHello but with different random value [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

D — Alert Injection

TLS MEDIUM 12 scenarios

Injects TLS alert records at incorrect points in the handshake state machine. Tests include sending warning alerts during handshake, fatal alerts followed by continued data, close_notify mid-handshake, unknown alert types, and alert floods.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 alert-during-handshake-small-ch Send warning alert between ClientHello and CKE [small CH] client DROPPED
2 alert-during-handshake-pqc-ch Send warning alert between ClientHello and CKE [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 fatal-alert-then-continue-small-ch Send fatal alert then continue handshake as if nothing happened [small CH] client DROPPED
4 fatal-alert-then-continue-pqc-ch Send fatal alert then continue handshake as if nothing happened [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 close-notify-mid-handshake-small-ch Send close_notify then continue with more messages [small CH] client DROPPED
6 close-notify-mid-handshake-pqc-ch Send close_notify then continue with more messages [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 unknown-alert-type-small-ch Send alert with undefined description code (255) [small CH] client DROPPED
8 unknown-alert-type-pqc-ch Send alert with undefined description code (255) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 alert-flood-small-ch Rapid-fire 20 warning alerts [small CH] client DROPPED
10 alert-flood-pqc-ch Rapid-fire 20 warning alerts [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 alert-wrong-level-small-ch Send handshake_failure with warning level instead of fatal [small CH] client DROPPED
12 alert-wrong-level-pqc-ch Send handshake_failure with warning level instead of fatal [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

E — TCP Manipulation

TLS LOW 10 scenarios

Attacks the TCP layer beneath the TLS handshake. Sends TCP FIN after ClientHello, RST mid-handshake, performs half-close then continues sending, drips ClientHello byte-by-byte (1 byte per 20ms), and fragments TLS records across TCP segments.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 fin-after-client-hello-small-ch Send ClientHello, then TCP FIN, then try to continue [small CH] client DROPPED
2 fin-after-client-hello-pqc-ch Send ClientHello, then TCP FIN, then try to continue [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 rst-mid-handshake-small-ch Send ClientHello, receive response, then TCP RST [small CH] client DROPPED
4 rst-mid-handshake-pqc-ch Send ClientHello, receive response, then TCP RST [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 half-close-continue-small-ch Half-close write side then send more TLS records [small CH] client DROPPED
6 half-close-continue-pqc-ch Half-close write side then send more TLS records [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 slow-drip-client-hello-small-ch Send ClientHello 1 byte at a time with delays [small CH] client DROPPED
8 slow-drip-client-hello-pqc-ch Send ClientHello 1 byte at a time with delays [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 split-record-across-segments-small-ch Fragment a ClientHello TLS record across 10 TCP segments [small CH] client DROPPED
10 split-record-across-segments-pqc-ch Fragment a ClientHello TLS record across 10 TCP segments [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

F — Record Layer Attacks

TLS HIGH 22 scenarios

Targets the TLS record layer with malformed records. Includes wrong record version fields, oversized records (>16KB), zero-length records, wrong content types, incorrect length fields, interleaved content types, and garbage data between valid records.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 tls13-strict-record-version-12-small-ch TLS 1.3 ClientHello using Record Version 0x0303 (TLS 1.2) instead of 0x0301 (Legacy) [small CH] client DROPPED
2 tls13-strict-record-version-12-pqc-ch TLS 1.3 ClientHello using Record Version 0x0303 (TLS 1.2) instead of 0x0301 (Legacy) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 tls13-strict-record-version-13-small-ch TLS 1.3 ClientHello using Record Version 0x0304 (TLS 1.3) — often dropped by middleboxes [small CH] client DROPPED
4 tls13-strict-record-version-13-pqc-ch TLS 1.3 ClientHello using Record Version 0x0304 (TLS 1.3) — often dropped by middleboxes [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 tls13-record-version-garbage-small-ch TLS 1.3 ClientHello using undefined Record Version (0x0305) [small CH] client DROPPED
6 tls13-record-version-garbage-pqc-ch TLS 1.3 ClientHello using undefined Record Version (0x0305) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 oversized-record-small-ch Send a TLS record > 16384 bytes [small CH] client DROPPED
8 oversized-record-pqc-ch Send a TLS record > 16384 bytes [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 zero-length-record-small-ch Send a TLS record with empty payload [small CH] client DROPPED
10 zero-length-record-pqc-ch Send a TLS record with empty payload [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 wrong-content-type-small-ch Send handshake data with application_data content type [small CH] client DROPPED
12 wrong-content-type-pqc-ch Send handshake data with application_data content type [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
13 wrong-record-length-small-ch TLS record length field doesn't match actual payload [small CH] client DROPPED
14 wrong-record-length-pqc-ch TLS record length field doesn't match actual payload [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
15 interleaved-content-types-small-ch Mix handshake and application_data records during handshake [small CH] client DROPPED
16 interleaved-content-types-pqc-ch Mix handshake and application_data records during handshake [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
17 record-version-mismatch-small-ch Record header says TLS 1.0, ClientHello body says TLS 1.2 [small CH] client DROPPED
18 record-version-mismatch-pqc-ch Record header says TLS 1.0, ClientHello body says TLS 1.2 [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
19 multiple-handshakes-one-record-small-ch Pack ClientHello + ClientKeyExchange in a single TLS record [small CH] client DROPPED
20 multiple-handshakes-one-record-pqc-ch Pack ClientHello + ClientKeyExchange in a single TLS record [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
21 garbage-between-records-small-ch Random garbage bytes between valid TLS records [small CH] client DROPPED
22 garbage-between-records-pqc-ch Random garbage bytes between valid TLS records [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

FV — Functional Validation (TLS)

TLS INFO 12 scenarios

Functional validation baselines — complete, correct TLS handshakes followed by real HTTP GET/POST traffic. These are non-fuzzing scenarios that establish ground truth for the target's normal operation.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 fv-tls-get-small-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + HTTP GET request, validate 200 OK response [small CH] client PASSED
2 fv-tls-get-pqc-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + HTTP GET request, validate 200 OK response [PQC big CH] client PASSED
3 fv-tls-post-small-small-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + HTTP POST with 256-byte body, validate echo [small CH] client PASSED
4 fv-tls-post-small-pqc-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + HTTP POST with 256-byte body, validate echo [PQC big CH] client PASSED
5 fv-tls-post-large-small-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + HTTP POST with 64KB body, validate echo [small CH] client PASSED
6 fv-tls-post-large-pqc-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + HTTP POST with 64KB body, validate echo [PQC big CH] client PASSED
7 fv-tls-sequential-requests-small-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + 10 sequential HTTP GET requests on same connection [small CH] client PASSED
8 fv-tls-sequential-requests-pqc-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + 10 sequential HTTP GET requests on same connection [PQC big CH] client PASSED
9 fv-tls-head-request-small-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + HTTP HEAD request, validate empty body with headers [small CH] client PASSED
10 fv-tls-head-request-pqc-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + HTTP HEAD request, validate empty body with headers [PQC big CH] client PASSED
11 fv-tls-post-then-get-small-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + POST then GET on same connection, validate both responses [small CH] client PASSED
12 fv-tls-post-then-get-pqc-ch Functional: full TLS handshake + POST then GET on same connection, validate both responses [PQC big CH] client PASSED

FW — Firewall Detection (TLS)

TLS HIGH 104 scenarios

Sends 104 known malicious payloads (malware signatures, SQL injection, XSS, command injection, exploit code, etc.) as HTTP POST bodies over TLS. The target firewall/IPS should detect and block each payload. Payload echoed back unchanged means detection failure.

Show all 104 scenarios
# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 fw-eicar-standard Firewall: EICAR standard test file — Standard EICAR antivirus test string (68 bytes) client DROPPED
2 fw-eicar-in-http-response Firewall: EICAR in HTTP response body — EICAR test file embedded in HTML page response client DROPPED
3 fw-eicar-base64 Firewall: EICAR base64 encoded — EICAR test file base64 encoded in POST body client DROPPED

104 fw-phishing-oauth-redirect Firewall: OAuth phishing redirect — Fake OAuth consent page redirecting credentials client DROPPED

G — ChangeCipherSpec Attacks

TLS HIGH 8 scenarios

Injects ChangeCipherSpec (CCS) records at illegal points in the handshake. Tests early CCS (before ServerHelloDone), multiple CCS records, CCS before any ClientHello, and CCS with embedded payload data. Based on the CCS Injection attack (CVE-2014-0224).

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 early-ccs-small-ch Send ChangeCipherSpec before receiving ServerHelloDone [small CH] client DROPPED
2 early-ccs-pqc-ch Send ChangeCipherSpec before receiving ServerHelloDone [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 multiple-ccs-small-ch Send ChangeCipherSpec three times in a row [small CH] client DROPPED
4 multiple-ccs-pqc-ch Send ChangeCipherSpec three times in a row [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 ccs-before-client-hello-small-ch Send ChangeCipherSpec as the very first message [small CH] client DROPPED
6 ccs-before-client-hello-pqc-ch Send ChangeCipherSpec as the very first message [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 ccs-with-payload-small-ch ChangeCipherSpec record with extra garbage bytes [small CH] client DROPPED
8 ccs-with-payload-pqc-ch ChangeCipherSpec record with extra garbage bytes [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

H — Extension Fuzzing

TLS MEDIUM 10 scenarios

Fuzzes TLS ClientHello extensions with structural errors. Tests duplicate extension IDs, unknown/undefined extension types (0xFEED, 0xBEEF), oversized extensions (64KB), empty SNI values, and malformed supported_versions.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 duplicate-extensions-small-ch ClientHello with the same extension type twice [small CH] client DROPPED
2 duplicate-extensions-pqc-ch ClientHello with the same extension type twice [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 unknown-extensions-small-ch ClientHello with unregistered extension type IDs [small CH] client DROPPED
4 unknown-extensions-pqc-ch ClientHello with unregistered extension type IDs [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 oversized-extension-small-ch ClientHello with a 64KB extension [small CH] client DROPPED
6 oversized-extension-pqc-ch ClientHello with a 64KB extension [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 empty-sni-small-ch ClientHello with empty SNI hostname [small CH] client DROPPED
8 empty-sni-pqc-ch ClientHello with empty SNI hostname [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 malformed-supported-versions-small-ch ClientHello with garbage data in supported_versions extension [small CH] client DROPPED
10 malformed-supported-versions-pqc-ch ClientHello with garbage data in supported_versions extension [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

I — Known Vulnerability Detection (CVEs)

TLS CRITICAL 32 scenarios

Probes for 32 known TLS/SSL CVEs including Heartbleed (CVE-2014-0160), POODLE (CVE-2014-3566), CCS Injection (CVE-2014-0224), FREAK (CVE-2015-0204), Logjam (CVE-2015-4000), DROWN (CVE-2016-0800), Sweet32 (CVE-2016-2183), CRIME (CVE-2012-4929), RC4 Bias, BEAST, Ticketbleed, and more.

Show all 32 scenarios
# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 heartbleed-cve-2014-0160-small-ch Heartbleed: send heartbeat with oversized payload_length to leak memory [small CH] client DROPPED

30 des-weak-cipher-pqc-ch Offer only DES cipher (56-bit key, trivially breakable) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

J — Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC)

TLS LOW 16 scenarios

Tests Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) key exchange support and robustness. Includes X25519+ML-KEM-768 hybrid (1216B), standalone ML-KEM-768, X25519Kyber768 draft format, malformed PQC key shares, oversized shares (10KB), and ML-KEM-1024.

4 pqc-standalone-mlkem768-pqc-ch Send ClientHello with standalone ML-KEM-768 key share (1184 bytes) [PQC big CH] client PASSED

6 pqc-kyber-draft-chrome-pqc-ch Send ClientHello with X25519Kyber768 draft group ID (Chrome experimental) [PQC big CH] client PASSED

8 pqc-malformed-key-share-pqc-ch Send PQC key share with wrong size (should be 1184, send 100) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

12 pqc-multiple-key-shares-pqc-ch Send multiple PQC key shares: hybrid + standalone + classical [PQC big CH] client PASSED

14 pqc-unknown-group-ids-pqc-ch Advertise only unregistered PQC named group IDs [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

K — SNI Evasion & Fragmentation

TLS MEDIUM 16 scenarios

Evades SNI-based Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) by fragmenting the ClientHello so that the SNI hostname is not in the first TCP segment. Techniques include delayed SNI, hostname-boundary splits, 1-byte TCP fragments, multiple SNI entries, IP addresses in SNI, and oversized hostnames.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 sni-not-in-first-packet-small-ch Fragment ClientHello so SNI hostname is in the 2nd TCP segment [small CH] client DROPPED
2 sni-not-in-first-packet-pqc-ch Fragment ClientHello so SNI hostname is in the 2nd TCP segment [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 sni-split-at-hostname-small-ch Split the ClientHello right in the middle of the SNI hostname string [small CH] client DROPPED
4 sni-split-at-hostname-pqc-ch Split the ClientHello right in the middle of the SNI hostname string [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 sni-tiny-fragments-small-ch Fragment ClientHello into 1-byte TCP segments to evade SNI inspection [small CH] client DROPPED
6 sni-tiny-fragments-pqc-ch Fragment ClientHello into 1-byte TCP segments to evade SNI inspection [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 sni-multiple-hostnames-small-ch SNI extension with multiple server_name entries (different hostnames) [small CH] client DROPPED
8 sni-multiple-hostnames-pqc-ch SNI extension with multiple server_name entries (different hostnames) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 sni-ip-address-small-ch SNI extension with an IP address instead of hostname [small CH] client DROPPED
10 sni-ip-address-pqc-ch SNI extension with an IP address instead of hostname [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 sni-oversized-hostname-small-ch SNI with extremely long hostname (500 chars) [small CH] client DROPPED
12 sni-oversized-hostname-pqc-ch SNI with extremely long hostname (500 chars) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
13 sni-record-header-fragment-small-ch Send only the 5-byte TLS record header first, then the rest [small CH] client DROPPED
14 sni-record-header-fragment-pqc-ch Send only the 5-byte TLS record header first, then the rest [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
15 sni-prepend-garbage-record-small-ch Send a garbage TLS record before the real ClientHello to confuse parsers [small CH] client DROPPED
16 sni-prepend-garbage-record-pqc-ch Send a garbage TLS record before the real ClientHello to confuse parsers [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

TLS MEDIUM 12 scenarios

Fuzzes ALPN (Application-Layer Protocol Negotiation) with invalid protocol identifiers. Tests unknown protocols, empty protocol strings, oversized lists (50 entries), duplicate "h2" entries, 255-byte protocol names, and wrong list length fields.

6 alpn-oversized-list-pqc-ch ClientHello with ALPN listing 50 protocol entries [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

12 alpn-wrong-list-length-pqc-ch ALPN extension with protocol_name_list length exceeding actual data [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

M — Extension Malformation & Placement

TLS MEDIUM 22 scenarios

Attacks the internal structure of individual TLS extensions. Sends extensions with wrong inner length fields (too short, too long), truncated key_share payloads, supported_versions with odd byte counts, and empty signature_algorithms.

N — TCP/TLS Parameter Reneging

TLS HIGH 20 scenarios

Tests "parameter reneging" — changing negotiated parameters between handshake messages. Includes cipher suite downgrades mid-handshake, version switches between ClientHello and Finished, and conflicting parameter values across messages.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 ccs-then-plaintext-handshake-small-ch Send CCS (signaling cipher activated) then send Finished as plaintext [small CH] client DROPPED

8 renegotiation-drop-extensions-pqc-ch Initial ClientHello with all extensions, renegotiation strips renegotiation_info and security extensions [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

18 record-version-renege-post-hello-pqc-ch ClientHello record says TLS 1.0 (normal), all subsequent records say TLS 1.3 [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

20 compression-renege-post-negotiation-pqc-ch Offer NULL compression initially, then renegotiation ClientHello offers DEFLATE [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

O — TLS 1.3 Early Data & 0-RTT Fuzzing

TLS HIGH 24 scenarios

Fuzzes TLS 1.3 early data (0-RTT) mechanisms. Sends early data without proper PSK, replays 0-RTT data, binds PSK to wrong identities, and manipulates the early_exporter_master_secret.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected

10 tls13-psk-identity-overflow-pqc-ch PSK identity with length field claiming more bytes than provided [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 tls13-early-data-oversized-small-ch Send 100KB of garbage as early application data (exceeds typical max_early_data_size) [small CH] client DROPPED
12 tls13-early-data-oversized-pqc-ch Send 100KB of garbage as early application data (exceeds typical max_early_data_size) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

14 tls13-early-data-before-client-hello-pqc-ch Send application data records BEFORE the ClientHello message [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
15 tls13-multiple-psk-binders-mismatch-small-ch PSK extension with 2 identities but 3 binders (count mismatch) [small CH] client DROPPED
16 tls13-multiple-psk-binders-mismatch-pqc-ch PSK extension with 2 identities but 3 binders (count mismatch) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

18 tls13-early-data-wrong-version-pqc-ch Early data records with SSL 3.0 version in record header [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

21 tls13-end-of-early-data-without-early-data-small-ch Send EndOfEarlyData handshake message without having sent early_data extension [small CH] client DROPPED
22 tls13-end-of-early-data-without-early-data-pqc-ch Send EndOfEarlyData handshake message without having sent early_data extension [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

24 tls13-early-data-after-finished-pqc-ch Send early data (application data records) AFTER sending Finished message [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

P — Advanced Handshake Record Fuzzing

TLS HIGH 26 scenarios

Advanced attacks on TLS record/handshake message boundaries. Packs multiple handshake messages into a single record, splits one handshake across many records, fragments handshake messages mid-field, and sends out-of-sequence fragments.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 handshake-fragmented-across-records-small-ch Split one ClientHello handshake message body across two separate TLS handshake records [small CH] client DROPPED
2 handshake-fragmented-across-records-pqc-ch Split one ClientHello handshake message body across two separate TLS handshake records [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 handshake-length-overflow-small-ch Handshake message with length field set to 0xFFFFFF (16MB) but only sending tiny body [small CH] client DROPPED
4 handshake-length-overflow-pqc-ch Handshake message with length field set to 0xFFFFFF (16MB) but only sending tiny body [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 handshake-length-underflow-small-ch Handshake length field = 10 but body is 200+ bytes [small CH] client DROPPED
6 handshake-length-underflow-pqc-ch Handshake length field = 10 but body is 200+ bytes [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 handshake-body-zero-length-small-ch ClientHello with handshake length = 0 (just the 4-byte header, no body) [small CH] client DROPPED
8 handshake-body-zero-length-pqc-ch ClientHello with handshake length = 0 (just the 4-byte header, no body) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 unknown-handshake-type-small-ch Send handshake message with type 99 (undefined in spec) [small CH] client DROPPED
10 unknown-handshake-type-pqc-ch Send handshake message with type 99 (undefined in spec) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 handshake-trailing-garbage-small-ch Valid ClientHello handshake record followed by 50 garbage bytes in the same TLS record [small CH] client DROPPED
12 handshake-trailing-garbage-pqc-ch Valid ClientHello handshake record followed by 50 garbage bytes in the same TLS record [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
13 handshake-header-only-no-body-small-ch Send just a 4-byte handshake header (Finished type + length=0) after valid ClientHello [small CH] client DROPPED
14 handshake-header-only-no-body-pqc-ch Send just a 4-byte handshake header (Finished type + length=0) after valid ClientHello [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
15 handshake-split-at-header-small-ch First TLS record contains only the 4-byte handshake header, second record contains the body [small CH] client DROPPED
16 handshake-split-at-header-pqc-ch First TLS record contains only the 4-byte handshake header, second record contains the body [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
17 triple-handshake-one-record-small-ch Pack ClientHello + CKE + Finished into a single TLS record [small CH] client DROPPED
18 triple-handshake-one-record-pqc-ch Pack ClientHello + CKE + Finished into a single TLS record [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
19 handshake-length-exceeds-record-small-ch Handshake msg_length > TLS record payload length (claims 500 bytes, record has 100) [small CH] client DROPPED
20 handshake-length-exceeds-record-pqc-ch Handshake msg_length > TLS record payload length (claims 500 bytes, record has 100) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
21 interleaved-handshake-and-alert-small-ch Alternate handshake fragments with alert records between them [small CH] client DROPPED
22 interleaved-handshake-and-alert-pqc-ch Alternate handshake fragments with alert records between them [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
23 handshake-type-zero-small-ch Send handshake message with type=0 (HelloRequest in TLS 1.2, unusual as client) [small CH] client DROPPED
24 handshake-type-zero-pqc-ch Send handshake message with type=0 (HelloRequest in TLS 1.2, unusual as client) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
25 handshake-message-max-type-small-ch Send handshake message with type=255 (maximum value) [small CH] client DROPPED
26 handshake-message-max-type-pqc-ch Send handshake message with type=255 (maximum value) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

PAN — PAN-OS URL Category SNI Probes

TLS INFO 810 scenarios

Palo Alto Networks URL category detection probes. Sends TLS ClientHello with SNI set to known domains from 30 URL categories (adult, malware, phishing, gambling, etc.) to verify PAN-OS URL filtering policy enforcement.

PAN-PQC — PAN-OS PQC + SNI Evasion Probes

TLS INFO 324 scenarios

Combines PQC key exchange bloat (~2KB ClientHello) with SNI fragmentation to test whether PAN-OS can still categorize URLs when the SNI hostname is delayed to the second TLS record or split across record boundaries.

Show all 324 scenarios
# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 pan-pqc-sni-delayed-adult-1 Large PQC ClientHello (~2KB). SNI (pornhub.com) pushed to 2nd TLS record. client PASSED
2 pan-pqc-sni-split-adult-1 Large PQC ClientHello. SNI (pornhub.com) split exactly across two TLS records. client PASSED
3 pan-pqc-sni-delayed-adult-2 Large PQC ClientHello (~2KB). SNI (xvideos.com) pushed to 2nd TLS record. client PASSED
4 pan-pqc-sni-split-adult-2 Large PQC ClientHello. SNI (xvideos.com) split exactly across two TLS records. client PASSED
5 pan-pqc-sni-delayed-adult-3 Large PQC ClientHello (~2KB). SNI (xnxx.com) pushed to 2nd TLS record. client PASSED
6 pan-pqc-sni-split-adult-3 Large PQC ClientHello. SNI (xnxx.com) split exactly across two TLS records. client PASSED
7 pan-pqc-sni-delayed-search-engines-1 Large PQC ClientHello (~2KB). SNI (google.com) pushed to 2nd TLS record. client PASSED
8 pan-pqc-sni-split-search-engines-1 Large PQC ClientHello. SNI (google.com) split exactly across two TLS records. client PASSED
9 pan-pqc-sni-delayed-search-engines-2 Large PQC ClientHello (~2KB). SNI (bing.com) pushed to 2nd TLS record. client PASSED
10 pan-pqc-sni-split-search-engines-2 Large PQC ClientHello. SNI (bing.com) split exactly across two TLS records. client PASSED

12 pan-pqc-sni-split-search-engines-3 Large PQC ClientHello. SNI (yahoo.com) split exactly across two TLS records. client PASSED

14 pan-pqc-sni-split-social-networking-1 Large PQC ClientHello. SNI (facebook.com) split exactly across two TLS records. client PASSED

Q — ClientHello Field Mutations

TLS MEDIUM 24 scenarios

Mutates individual fields within the ClientHello message body. Attacks session ID length/content, cipher suite list ordering and padding, compression method fields, random value bytes, and supported_versions list entries.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 ch-session-id-zero-length-small-ch ClientHello with session_id length = 0 (empty session ID) [small CH] client DROPPED
2 ch-session-id-zero-length-pqc-ch ClientHello with session_id length = 0 (empty session ID) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 ch-session-id-oversized-small-ch ClientHello with 255-byte session ID (exceeds 32-byte max per RFC) [small CH] client DROPPED
4 ch-session-id-oversized-pqc-ch ClientHello with 255-byte session ID (exceeds 32-byte max per RFC) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 ch-session-id-length-mismatch-small-ch Session ID length field says 32 but only 8 bytes of data follow [small CH] client DROPPED
6 ch-session-id-length-mismatch-pqc-ch Session ID length field says 32 but only 8 bytes of data follow [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 ch-cipher-suites-empty-small-ch ClientHello with cipher_suites length = 0 (no ciphers offered) [small CH] client DROPPED
8 ch-cipher-suites-empty-pqc-ch ClientHello with cipher_suites length = 0 (no ciphers offered) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 ch-cipher-suites-odd-length-small-ch ClientHello with cipher_suites length = 3 (odd, not multiple of 2) [small CH] client DROPPED
10 ch-cipher-suites-odd-length-pqc-ch ClientHello with cipher_suites length = 3 (odd, not multiple of 2) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 ch-cipher-suites-length-overflow-small-ch Cipher suites length claims 1000 but only 26 bytes of data follow [small CH] client DROPPED
12 ch-cipher-suites-length-overflow-pqc-ch Cipher suites length claims 1000 but only 26 bytes of data follow [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
13 ch-compression-invalid-methods-small-ch ClientHello with invalid compression methods [DEFLATE, 0x40, 0xFE, 0xFF] [small CH] client DROPPED
14 ch-compression-invalid-methods-pqc-ch ClientHello with invalid compression methods [DEFLATE, 0x40, 0xFE, 0xFF] [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
15 ch-compression-empty-small-ch ClientHello with compression_methods length = 0 (none offered) [small CH] client DROPPED
16 ch-compression-empty-pqc-ch ClientHello with compression_methods length = 0 (none offered) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
17 ch-version-undefined-small-ch ClientHello with client_version = 0x0000 (completely undefined) [small CH] client DROPPED
18 ch-version-undefined-pqc-ch ClientHello with client_version = 0x0000 (completely undefined) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
19 ch-version-future-small-ch ClientHello with client_version = 0x0305 (hypothetical TLS 1.4) [small CH] client DROPPED
20 ch-version-future-pqc-ch ClientHello with client_version = 0x0305 (hypothetical TLS 1.4) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
21 ch-random-all-zeros-small-ch ClientHello with random field = 32 bytes of 0x00 (deterministic) [small CH] client DROPPED
22 ch-random-all-zeros-pqc-ch ClientHello with random field = 32 bytes of 0x00 (deterministic) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
23 ch-extensions-length-zero-with-data-small-ch Extensions total length field = 0 but real extension data follows [small CH] client DROPPED
24 ch-extensions-length-zero-with-data-pqc-ch Extensions total length field = 0 but real extension data follows [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

R — Extension Inner Structure Fuzzing

TLS MEDIUM 28 scenarios

Fuzzes the inner byte structure of TLS extensions. Attacks key_share format at the byte level, supported_groups encoding, signature_algorithms truncation, and extension length field off-by-one errors.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 ext-sni-name-type-invalid-small-ch SNI extension with name_type = 0xFF instead of 0x00 (host_name) [small CH] client DROPPED
2 ext-sni-name-type-invalid-pqc-ch SNI extension with name_type = 0xFF instead of 0x00 (host_name) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 ext-sni-list-length-overflow-small-ch SNI server_name_list_length claims 500 bytes but actual list is ~20 bytes [small CH] client DROPPED
4 ext-sni-list-length-overflow-pqc-ch SNI server_name_list_length claims 500 bytes but actual list is ~20 bytes [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 ext-sni-hostname-null-bytes-small-ch SNI hostname with embedded null byte: "exam\x00ple.com" [small CH] client DROPPED
6 ext-sni-hostname-null-bytes-pqc-ch SNI hostname with embedded null byte: "exam\x00ple.com" [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 ext-supported-groups-empty-list-small-ch supported_groups extension with list_length = 0 (empty group list) [small CH] client DROPPED
8 ext-supported-groups-empty-list-pqc-ch supported_groups extension with list_length = 0 (empty group list) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

S — Record Layer Byte Attacks

TLS MEDIUM 16 scenarios

Byte-level attacks on TLS record headers. Flips individual bits in version bytes, varies content type values, introduces off-by-one in length fields, and tests version/length field boundary conditions.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 record-content-type-zero-small-ch TLS record with content_type = 0x00 (undefined) wrapping valid CH [small CH] client DROPPED

3 record-content-type-max-small-ch TLS record with content_type = 0xFF (max value) wrapping valid CH [small CH] client DROPPED

5 record-content-type-25-small-ch TLS record with content_type = 25 (first undefined after HEARTBEAT=24) [small CH] client DROPPED
6 record-content-type-25-pqc-ch TLS record with content_type = 25 (first undefined after HEARTBEAT=24) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 record-version-zero-small-ch TLS record with version = 0x0000 wrapping valid ClientHello [small CH] client DROPPED
8 record-version-zero-pqc-ch TLS record with version = 0x0000 wrapping valid ClientHello [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 record-version-max-small-ch TLS record with version = 0xFFFF wrapping valid ClientHello [small CH] client DROPPED
10 record-version-max-pqc-ch TLS record with version = 0xFFFF wrapping valid ClientHello [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 record-length-one-byte-small-ch TLS record with 1-byte payload (truncated handshake data) [small CH] client DROPPED
12 record-length-one-byte-pqc-ch TLS record with 1-byte payload (truncated handshake data) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

SB — Sandbox Detection (TLS)

TLS HIGH 55 scenarios

Sends 55 sandbox-evasion payloads (exploit kit landing pages, obfuscated JavaScript, browser exploits, cryptocurrency miners, droppers) to a well-behaved echo server. The firewall should detect malicious content in the response body and block it.

Show all 55 scenarios

SCAN — TLS Compatibility Scanning (Non-fuzzing)

TLS INFO 214 scenarios

Non-fuzzing compatibility scanning. Tests every combination of TLS version (SSL 3.0 through TLS 1.3), cipher suite, named group, and signature algorithm to map the target's supported configuration matrix.

Show all 214 scenarios

SRV — TLS Server-Side Fuzzing

TLS HIGH 33 scenarios

Server-side TLS fuzzing — the fuzzer acts as a malicious TLS server. Sends invalid ServerHello, premature CCS, wrong cipher suite selection, corrupted certificates, and truncated handshake messages to connecting clients.

Show all 33 scenarios
# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 server-hello-before-client-hello Server sends ServerHello immediately without waiting for ClientHello server DROPPED
2 duplicate-server-hello Server sends ServerHello twice server DROPPED
3 skip-server-hello-done Server omits ServerHelloDone server DROPPED
4 certificate-before-server-hello Server sends Certificate before ServerHello server DROPPED
5 double-server-hello-done Server sends ServerHelloDone twice server DROPPED
6 cipher-suite-mismatch Server selects a cipher suite not in client's offered list server DROPPED
7 compression-method-mismatch Server picks DEFLATE compression when client only offered NULL server DROPPED
8 fin-after-server-hello Server sends ServerHello then TCP FIN then continues server DROPPED
9 fin-from-both Server sends FIN immediately after ServerHello, simulating simultaneous FIN server DROPPED
10 alpn-mismatch-server Server selects ALPN "h2" when client only offered "http/1.1" server DROPPED
11 cert-expired Server certificate with notAfter in the past (expired 2001) server DROPPED
12 cert-not-yet-valid Server certificate with notBefore in the future (2040) server DROPPED
13 cert-sig-algorithm-mismatch Certificate with mismatched signature algorithms: tbsCert=SHA256/RSA, outer=ECDSA/SHA256 server DROPPED
14 cert-signature-all-zeros Certificate with signatureValue = 256 bytes of 0x00 server DROPPED
15 cert-signature-truncated Certificate with signatureValue = 1 byte only server DROPPED
16 cert-serial-negative Certificate with negative serial number (leading 0xFF byte) server DROPPED
17 cert-serial-zero Certificate with serial number = 0 server DROPPED
18 cert-subject-empty Certificate with empty subject DN (no RDN sequences) server DROPPED
19 cert-cn-null-byte Certificate with CN containing null byte: "evil.com\x00.good.com" server DROPPED
20 cert-wildcard-bare Certificate with CN = "*" (bare wildcard, no domain restriction) server DROPPED
21 cert-san-null-byte Certificate with SAN dNSName containing null byte: "evil.com\x00.good.com" server DROPPED
22 cert-v1-with-extensions Certificate version=v1 but includes v3 extensions (invalid per X.509) server DROPPED
23 cert-version-invalid Certificate with version=v4 (3) — only v1/v2/v3 are defined server DROPPED
24 cert-pubkey-zero-length Certificate with SubjectPublicKeyInfo containing 0-byte key data server DROPPED
25 cert-critical-unknown-ext Certificate with critical=TRUE unknown extension OID (must reject) server DROPPED
26 cert-chain-100-depth Certificate chain with 100 small certificates (chain depth attack) server DROPPED
27 cert-chain-length-overflow Certificate message with certificates_length claiming 10000, actual ~500 bytes server DROPPED
28 cert-chain-length-underflow Certificate message with certificates_length claiming 10, actual ~500 bytes server DROPPED
29 cert-entry-zero-length Certificate chain with a cert entry whose length field = 0 server DROPPED
30 cert-entry-length-overflow Certificate entry with cert_length claiming 5000 but only 200 bytes follow server DROPPED
31 cert-chain-trailing-garbage Valid certificate chain with 100 bytes of trailing garbage in message server DROPPED
32 cert-chain-single-byte-entries 50 certificate entries of 1 byte each (minimal entries) server DROPPED
33 cert-message-max-size Certificate message with certificates_length claiming ~16MB (near max handshake length) server DROPPED

T — Alert & CCS Byte-Level Fuzzing

TLS MEDIUM 20 scenarios

Byte-level fuzzing of TLS Alert and ChangeCipherSpec messages. Flips alert level/description bits, injects payload into CCS records, sends multiple CCS bytes, and corrupts alert record format.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 alert-level-zero-small-ch Alert message with level=0 (undefined, below WARNING=1) [small CH] client DROPPED
2 alert-level-zero-pqc-ch Alert message with level=0 (undefined, below WARNING=1) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 alert-level-max-small-ch Alert message with level=255 (undefined, above FATAL=2) [small CH] client DROPPED
4 alert-level-max-pqc-ch Alert message with level=255 (undefined, above FATAL=2) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 alert-descriptions-undefined-small-ch Send alerts with 5 unused description codes: 1, 23, 55, 72, 200 [small CH] client DROPPED
6 alert-descriptions-undefined-pqc-ch Send alerts with 5 unused description codes: 1, 23, 55, 72, 200 [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 alert-record-truncated-small-ch Alert record with 1-byte payload (missing description byte) [small CH] client DROPPED
8 alert-record-truncated-pqc-ch Alert record with 1-byte payload (missing description byte) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 alert-record-oversized-small-ch Alert record with 100 bytes (98 trailing garbage bytes) [small CH] client DROPPED
10 alert-record-oversized-pqc-ch Alert record with 100 bytes (98 trailing garbage bytes) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 alert-record-empty-small-ch Alert record with 0-byte payload (empty alert) [small CH] client DROPPED
12 alert-record-empty-pqc-ch Alert record with 0-byte payload (empty alert) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
13 ccs-payload-zero-small-ch CCS with payload byte = 0x00 (must be 0x01 per spec) [small CH] client DROPPED
14 ccs-payload-zero-pqc-ch CCS with payload byte = 0x00 (must be 0x01 per spec) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
15 ccs-payload-two-small-ch CCS with payload byte = 0x02 (invalid) [small CH] client DROPPED
16 ccs-payload-two-pqc-ch CCS with payload byte = 0x02 (invalid) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
17 ccs-payload-ff-small-ch CCS with payload byte = 0xFF (invalid) [small CH] client DROPPED
18 ccs-payload-ff-pqc-ch CCS with payload byte = 0xFF (invalid) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
19 ccs-record-empty-small-ch CCS record with 0-byte payload (empty) [small CH] client DROPPED
20 ccs-record-empty-pqc-ch CCS record with 0-byte payload (empty) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

U — Handshake Type & Legacy Protocol Fuzzing

TLS MEDIUM 20 scenarios

Sends handshake messages with undefined or legacy type codes. Includes undefined types (0xFF), SSLv2 ClientHello format, heartbeat extension requests (Heartbleed vector), and SSLv3 remnant messages.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 hs-server-hello-from-client-small-ch Client sends ServerHello (handshake type 2) as first message — role violation [small CH] client DROPPED
2 hs-server-hello-from-client-pqc-ch Client sends ServerHello (handshake type 2) as first message — role violation [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
3 hs-certificate-unrequested-small-ch Client sends Certificate (handshake type 11) as first message — unrequested [small CH] client DROPPED
4 hs-certificate-unrequested-pqc-ch Client sends Certificate (handshake type 11) as first message — unrequested [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 hs-key-update-pre-encryption-small-ch Client sends KeyUpdate (handshake type 24) before encryption is established [small CH] client DROPPED
6 hs-key-update-pre-encryption-pqc-ch Client sends KeyUpdate (handshake type 24) before encryption is established [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 hs-undefined-types-batch-small-ch After valid CH, send 5 undefined handshake types: 3, 6, 7, 9, 10 [small CH] client DROPPED
8 hs-undefined-types-batch-pqc-ch After valid CH, send 5 undefined handshake types: 3, 6, 7, 9, 10 [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
9 sslv2-version-zero-small-ch SSLv2 ClientHello with version = 0x0000 (undefined) [small CH] client DROPPED
10 sslv2-version-zero-pqc-ch SSLv2 ClientHello with version = 0x0000 (undefined) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 sslv2-challenge-empty-small-ch SSLv2 ClientHello with challenge_length = 0 (empty challenge) [small CH] client DROPPED
12 sslv2-challenge-empty-pqc-ch SSLv2 ClientHello with challenge_length = 0 (empty challenge) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
13 sslv2-cipher-specs-invalid-small-ch SSLv2 ClientHello with all-zero cipher specs [small CH] client DROPPED
14 sslv2-cipher-specs-invalid-pqc-ch SSLv2 ClientHello with all-zero cipher specs [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
15 heartbeat-response-type-small-ch Heartbeat message with type=RESPONSE (2) instead of REQUEST (1) [small CH] client DROPPED
16 heartbeat-response-type-pqc-ch Heartbeat message with type=RESPONSE (2) instead of REQUEST (1) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
17 heartbeat-zero-payload-length-small-ch Heartbeat request with payload_length=0 [small CH] client DROPPED
18 heartbeat-zero-payload-length-pqc-ch Heartbeat request with payload_length=0 [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
19 heartbeat-no-padding-small-ch Heartbeat request with payload but 0 bytes padding (RFC requires >=16) [small CH] client DROPPED
20 heartbeat-no-padding-pqc-ch Heartbeat request with payload but 0 bytes padding (RFC requires >=16) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

V — Cipher Suite & Signature Algorithm Fuzzing

TLS MEDIUM 22 scenarios

Fuzzes cipher suite and signature algorithm values. Sends unknown cipher suite IDs, mismatched signature algorithms, unsupported signing algorithms, GREASE values (RFC 8701), and reordered cipher suite lists.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 cs-grease-values-small-ch ClientHello offering only GREASE cipher suites (0x0A0A, 0x1A1A, 0x2A2A, 0x3A3A) [small CH] client DROPPED
2 cs-grease-values-pqc-ch ClientHello offering only GREASE cipher suites (0x0A0A, 0x1A1A, 0x2A2A, 0x3A3A) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

4 cs-null-null-pqc-ch ClientHello offering only cipher suite 0x0000 (TLS_NULL_WITH_NULL_NULL) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
5 cs-max-value-small-ch ClientHello offering only cipher suite 0xFFFF (undefined maximum) [small CH] client DROPPED
6 cs-max-value-pqc-ch ClientHello offering only cipher suite 0xFFFF (undefined maximum) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
7 cs-scsv-only-small-ch ClientHello with only TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV (0x5600) as sole cipher [small CH] client DROPPED
8 cs-scsv-only-pqc-ch ClientHello with only TLS_FALLBACK_SCSV (0x5600) as sole cipher [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

10 cs-massive-list-pqc-ch ClientHello with 200 cipher suites (parser stress test) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
11 sig-algs-sha1-only-small-ch signature_algorithms with only SHA-1 variants (deprecated) [small CH] client DROPPED
12 sig-algs-sha1-only-pqc-ch signature_algorithms with only SHA-1 variants (deprecated) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED
13 sig-algs-zero-small-ch signature_algorithms with algorithm value 0x0000 (undefined) [small CH] client DROPPED
14 sig-algs-zero-pqc-ch signature_algorithms with algorithm value 0x0000 (undefined) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

22 groups-deprecated-pqc-ch supported_groups with deprecated curves (sect163k1=0x0001, sect163r2=0x0003) [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

X — Client Certificate Abuse

TLS MEDIUM 24 scenarios

Sends unsolicited or malformed client certificates. Tests include certificates sent without CertificateRequest, self-signed certs with wrong key sizes, expired certificates, and certificate validation bypass attempts.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 client-cert-unsolicited-post-hello-small-ch After CH→SH exchange, client sends Certificate without CertificateRequest [small CH] client DROPPED

12 client-cert-oversized-pqc-ch Client sends Certificate with 32KB of cert data [PQC big CH] client DROPPED

Z — Well-behaved Counterparts

TLS LOW 10 scenarios

Well-behaved counterparts that implement correct TLS behavior. Used internally for distributed mode testing to verify that compliant connections succeed. Includes standard TLS handshake and HTTP GET/POST over TLS.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 well-behaved-client Compliant TLS client handshake — used to interact with a fuzzed server client PASSED
2 app-post-64kb HTTP POST with 64KB body — at default TCP window boundary client PASSED
3 app-post-128kb HTTP POST with 128KB body — 2x default TCP receive window client PASSED
4 app-post-256kb HTTP POST with 256KB body — 4x default TCP receive window client PASSED
5 app-post-512kb HTTP POST with 512KB body — 8x default TCP receive window client PASSED
6 app-post-1mb HTTP POST with 1MB body — large transfer spanning many TCP segments client PASSED
7 app-post-2mb HTTP POST with 2MB body — very large transfer client PASSED
8 app-post-10mb HTTP POST with 10MB body — extreme sustained throughput test client PASSED
9 app-post-chunked-256kb HTTP POST with 256KB body using chunked Transfer-Encoding client PASSED
10 well-behaved-server Compliant TLS server handshake — used to interact with a fuzzed client server PASSED

AA — HTTP/2 CVE & Rapid Attack

HTTP/2 CRITICAL 2 scenarios

Tests critical HTTP/2 CVEs. Rapid Reset (CVE-2023-44487): opens 100 streams with immediate RST_STREAM to overwhelm the server. CONTINUATION Flood: sends 50 CONTINUATION frames without END_HEADERS to exhaust server header buffers.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-rapid-reset-cve-44487 Rapid Reset Attack (CVE-2023-44487) — 100 HEADERS+RST_STREAM pairs in rapid succession client DROPPED
2 h2-continuation-flood CONTINUATION Flood — HEADERS without END_HEADERS followed by 50 CONTINUATION frames client DROPPED

AB — HTTP/2 Flood / Resource Exhaustion

HTTP/2 HIGH 3 scenarios

Resource exhaustion attacks. SETTINGS flood (1000 frames), PING flood (1000 frames), and empty DATA frame flood (50 frames per stream). Tests server rate limiting and backpressure mechanisms.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-settings-flood SETTINGS Flood — sends 1000 SETTINGS frames to exhaust server ACK queue client DROPPED
2 h2-ping-flood PING Flood — sends 1000 PING frames to trigger 1000 PING ACK responses client DROPPED
3 h2-empty-frames-flood Empty DATA Frames Flood — 50 zero-length DATA frames on a single stream client DROPPED

AC — HTTP/2 Stream & Flow Control Violations

HTTP/2 HIGH 4 scenarios

Violates HTTP/2 stream and flow control rules. Opens 110 streams when the limit is 100, sends erratic WINDOW_UPDATE (zero increment, on closed streams, max overflow), exceeds initial flow control window, and creates circular PRIORITY dependencies.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-max-concurrent-streams-bypass Exceeds SETTINGS_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS — opens 110 streams beyond the default limit of 100 client DROPPED
2 h2-erratic-window-update Erratic WINDOW_UPDATE frames — zero increment, update on closed stream, max increment client DROPPED
3 h2-flow-control-violation Flow Control Violation — sends DATA exceeding initial connection flow control window (65535 bytes) client DROPPED
4 h2-priority-circular-dependency PRIORITY frame with circular self-dependency — stream depends on itself (RFC 7540 §5.3.1) client DROPPED

AD — HTTP/2 Frame Structure & Header Attacks

HTTP/2 MEDIUM 5 scenarios

Attacks HTTP/2 frame structure and headers. Sends SETTINGS on non-zero stream ID, HEADERS on stream 0, stray CONTINUATION without preceding HEADERS, undefined frame types (0xFF), HPACK bombs, and frames with wrong payload sizes.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-protocol-violation Protocol Violations — SETTINGS on non-zero stream, HEADERS on stream 0, stray CONTINUATION, undefined frame type, DATA on idle stream client DROPPED
2 h2-hpack-bomb HPACK Bomb — 100 unique headers exhausting the HPACK dynamic table client DROPPED
3 h2-invalid-header Invalid Header Fields — pseudo-header after regular header, invalid characters, oversized name client DROPPED
4 h2-invalid-frame-size Invalid Frame Size — SETTINGS with under-reported length, PING claiming wrong payload size client DROPPED
5 h2-padding-fuzz Padding Abuse — HEADERS with PADDED flag where declared length exceeds actual payload client DROPPED

AE — HTTP/2 Stream Abuse Extensions

HTTP/2 HIGH 2 scenarios

Extended stream abuse vectors including RST_STREAM flood (CVE-2019-9514) and dependency cycle attacks.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-reset-flood-cve-9514 Reset Flood (CVE-2019-9514) — DATA after END_STREAM on 50 streams to provoke RST_STREAM cascade client DROPPED
2 h2-dependency-cycle Stream Dependency Cycle — self-referencing PRIORITY on 10 streams and an A↔B cross-cycle client DROPPED

AF — HTTP/2 Extended Frame Attacks

HTTP/2 MEDIUM 7 scenarios

Malformed frame attacks: corrupted RST_STREAM payload, PUSH_PROMISE without SETTINGS enablement, invalid GOAWAY frames, and corrupted DATA payloads.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-malformed-settings-frame Malformed SETTINGS — SETTINGS frame with invalid identifier 0xFFFF client DROPPED
2 h2-large-headers-frame Large HEADERS Frame — ~200KB of header data (200 headers × 1KB each) client DROPPED
3 h2-zero-length-headers-cve-9516 Zero-Length Headers (CVE-2019-9516) — HEADERS with empty names and empty values client DROPPED
4 h2-continuation-flood-1000 Aggressive CONTINUATION Flood (CVE-2024-27316) — HEADERS without END_HEADERS + 1000 CONTINUATION frames client DROPPED
5 h2-invalid-frame-types Unknown Frame Types — frames with type codes 0x0A, 0x0B, 0x0F, 0x42, 0xFF on streams 0 and 1 client DROPPED
6 h2-connection-preface-attack Malformed Connection Preface — sends a truncated HTTP/2 client preface to test server handshake validation client DROPPED
7 h2-goaway-flood GOAWAY Flood — sends 10 GOAWAY frames with different error codes to test connection shutdown handling client DROPPED

AG — HTTP/2 Flow Control Attacks

HTTP/2 HIGH 4 scenarios

Flow control window manipulation. Exhausts connection-level windows, overflows increment counters, manipulates per-stream windows. Includes CVE-2019-9517 (internal data buffering).

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-flow-control-manipulation-cve-9517 Flow Control Manipulation (CVE-2019-9517) — maximize connection and stream windows, open 20 streams, never read responses client DROPPED
2 h2-window-overflow Window Overflow — two WINDOW_UPDATE increments of 0x7FFFFFFF on connection and stream 1 to exceed 2^31-1 client DROPPED
3 h2-zero-window-size-cve-43622 Zero Window Size (CVE-2023-43622) — SETTINGS with INITIAL_WINDOW_SIZE=0, then sends 20 requests server cannot respond to client DROPPED
4 h2-invalid-stream-states Invalid Stream States — DATA on idle stream, HEADERS on even stream ID, DATA on closed stream, zero-increment WINDOW_UPDATE client DROPPED

AH — HTTP/2 Connectivity & TLS Probes

HTTP/2 INFO 14 scenarios

Non-fuzzing HTTP/2 connectivity probes. Tests TCP reachability, TLS ALPN negotiation, HTTP/2 preface exchange, and SETTINGS round-trip across different TLS configurations.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-ping-tcp TCP Connectivity Probe — basic TCP connection test to verify host:port is reachable client CONNECTED
2 h2-ping-tls-alpn TLS + ALPN h2 Connectivity Probe — TLS connection test with ALPN h2 to verify HTTP/2 support client CONNECTED
3 h2-alpn-correct Correct ALPN (h2) — connect with correct h2 ALPN; should succeed client CONNECTED
4 h2-alpn-incorrect Incorrect ALPN (http/1.1) — connect with HTTP/1.1 only ALPN; should fail if server requires h2 client CONNECTED
5 h2-alpn-mixed Mixed ALPN (h2, http/1.1) — connect with both protocols; should succeed via h2 negotiation client CONNECTED
6 h2-alpn-empty Empty ALPN — connect with an empty ALPN list; should fail for strict HTTP/2 servers client CONNECTED
7 h2-alpn-random Random/Unknown ALPN — connect with a nonsense ALPN string; should fail client FAILED_CONNECTION
8 h2-alpn-missing Missing ALPN Extension — connect without any ALPN extension; should fail for HTTP/2 client CONNECTED
9 h2-tls-v12-only TLSv1.2 Only — force TLS 1.2; should succeed (HTTP/2 requires TLS 1.2+) client CONNECTED
10 h2-tls-v13-only TLSv1.3 Only — force TLS 1.3; should succeed if server supports it client CONNECTED
11 h2-tls-v11-only TLSv1.1 Only — force deprecated TLS 1.1; should fail (HTTP/2 requires 1.2+) client FAILED_CONNECTION
12 h2-tls-negotiate TLS Version Negotiation (1.2→1.3) — allow client and server to negotiate; should succeed client CONNECTED
13 well-behaved-h2-client Compliant HTTP/2 client — used to interact with a fuzzed server client PASSED
14 well-behaved-h2-server Compliant HTTP/2 server — used to interact with a fuzzed client server PASSED

AI — HTTP/2 General Frame Mutation

HTTP/2 LOW 1 scenario

Randomized frame mutation — sends frames with random type, flags, stream ID, and payload to test parser robustness against unexpected input.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-random-frame-mutation Random Frame Mutation — valid preface then mutated frames: unknown type, over-reported length, all flags set, even stream DATA, random garbage client DROPPED

AM — HTTP/2 Functional Validation

HTTP/2 INFO 5 scenarios

Functional validation baselines for HTTP/2. Performs normal GET and POST requests, proper stream lifecycle, clean SETTINGS exchange, and GOAWAY shutdown.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-fv-single-get Functional: HTTP/2 connection + single GET stream, validate 200 OK client PASSED
2 h2-fv-single-post Functional: HTTP/2 connection + single POST stream with 1KB body, validate echo client PASSED
3 h2-fv-multi-stream-get-100 Functional: HTTP/2 connection + 100 concurrent GET streams, validate all responses client PASSED
4 h2-fv-multi-stream-post-100 Functional: HTTP/2 connection + 100 concurrent POST streams with 512-byte bodies client PASSED
5 h2-fv-mixed-methods-100 Functional: HTTP/2 connection + 100 concurrent mixed GET/POST streams client PASSED

H2S — HTTP/2 Server-Side Fuzzing

HTTP/2 HIGH 28 scenarios

HTTP/2 server-side fuzzing — the fuzzer acts as a malicious server. Includes PUSH flood, malformed response headers, corrupted DATA frames, and invalid SETTINGS ACKs.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 h2-server-push-flood Server Push Flood — server sends 100 PUSH_PROMISE frames for a single client request server DROPPED
2 h2-server-malformed-response-headers Malformed Response Headers — server sends HEADERS response without required :status pseudo-header server DROPPED
3 h2-server-oversized-response-headers Oversized Response Headers — server sends response with ~200KB of custom headers (200 × 1KB) server DROPPED
4 h2-server-invalid-status-code Invalid Status Code — server sends :status 999 via raw HPACK to test client parsing server DROPPED
5 h2-server-goaway-abuse Server GOAWAY Abuse — sends GOAWAY with misleading last-stream-id values (0 and 0x7FFFFFFF) server DROPPED
6 h2-server-settings-manipulation Server Settings Manipulation — sends extreme SETTINGS: maxConcurrentStreams=0, window limits, disabled HPACK server DROPPED
7 h2-server-rst-stream-flood Server RST_STREAM Flood — server immediately RST_STREAMs every incoming request with rotating error codes server DROPPED
8 h2-server-continuation-flood Server CONTINUATION Flood — server sends fragmented response headers via 500 CONTINUATION frames server DROPPED
9 h2-server-data-after-end-stream Data After END_STREAM — server sends DATA frames after already ending the stream server DROPPED
10 h2-server-window-manipulation Server Window Manipulation — server sends zero-increment and overflow WINDOW_UPDATE frames server DROPPED
11 h2-server-settings-nonzero-stream SETTINGS on Non-Zero Stream — server sends SETTINGS on stream 1; RFC §6.5 requires stream 0 server DROPPED
12 h2-server-rst-stream-zero RST_STREAM on Stream 0 — server sends RST_STREAM on connection stream; RFC §6.4 requires stream ID > 0 server DROPPED
13 h2-server-data-stream-zero DATA on Stream 0 — server sends DATA frame on connection stream; only HEADERS/SETTINGS/etc. allowed on stream 0 server DROPPED
14 h2-server-headers-stream-zero HEADERS on Stream 0 — server sends HEADERS on connection stream; only valid on non-zero streams server DROPPED
15 h2-server-ping-nonzero-stream PING on Non-Zero Stream — server sends PING on stream 1; RFC §6.7 requires stream 0 server DROPPED
16 h2-server-goaway-nonzero-stream GOAWAY on Non-Zero Stream — server sends GOAWAY on stream 1; RFC §6.8 requires stream 0 server DROPPED
17 h2-server-push-promise-odd-stream PUSH_PROMISE with Odd Promised Stream ID — server promises stream 1 (odd); RFC §6.6 requires even server-initiated IDs server DROPPED
18 h2-server-continuation-no-headers Stray CONTINUATION — server sends CONTINUATION without a preceding HEADERS without END_HEADERS (RFC §6.10) server DROPPED
19 h2-server-unknown-frames Unknown Frame Types from Server — sends frames with undefined types (0x0B, 0x42, 0xFF); RFC §4.1 requires clients to ignore them server PASSED
20 h2-server-uppercase-header Uppercase Header Name — server response contains "X-Custom" (uppercase); HTTP/2 requires all header names to be lowercase server DROPPED
21 h2-server-connection-header Connection Header in Response — server sends "Connection: keep-alive"; connection-specific headers are forbidden in HTTP/2 server DROPPED
22 h2-server-transfer-encoding Transfer-Encoding in Response — server sends "Transfer-Encoding: chunked"; forbidden in HTTP/2 (§8.1.2.2) server DROPPED
23 h2-server-multiple-status Multiple :status Pseudo-Headers — server sends ":status 200" then ":status 404" in one HEADERS frame server DROPPED
24 h2-server-pseudo-after-regular Pseudo-Header After Regular Header — server sends a regular header before :status in the response server DROPPED
25 h2-server-request-pseudoheaders Request Pseudo-Headers in Response — server sends :method GET and :path / in a response HEADERS frame server DROPPED
26 h2-server-te-non-trailers TE: chunked in Response — server sends "TE: chunked"; HTTP/2 only allows "TE: trailers" (RFC §8.1.2.2) server DROPPED
27 h2-server-empty-status Empty :status Value — server sends :status with an empty string instead of a 3-digit code server DROPPED
28 h2-server-keep-alive-header Keep-Alive Header in Response — server sends "Keep-Alive: timeout=5"; connection-specific header forbidden in HTTP/2 server DROPPED

QA — QUIC Handshake & Connection Initial

QUIC INFO 9 scenarios

QUIC handshake and connection initialization fuzzing. Tests ClientHello ordering within CRYPTO frames, incomplete handshakes, duplicate Initial packets, version mismatches, and PQC key share injection.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 quic-0-rtt-fuzz 0-RTT Early Data packet with random payload to probe server replay handling client DROPPED
2 quic-pqc-keyshare QUIC Initial with ML-KEM (Kyber-768) sized CRYPTO frame to test PQC handling client DROPPED
3 quic-packet-coalescing Two QUIC Initial packets coalesced into a single UDP datagram client DROPPED
4 quic-handshake-initial Basic QUIC Initial packet with random payload client DROPPED
5 quic-version-negotiation QUIC Version Negotiation trigger — sends version 0 client DROPPED
6 quic-retry-token-fuzz QUIC Retry packet with random token and tag client DROPPED
7 well-behaved-quic-client Compliant QUIC client handshake baseline (via OpenSSL 3.6+) client PASSED
8 well-behaved-quic-client-100-streams Compliant QUIC client handshake and multiplexes 100 streams (via OpenSSL 3.6+) client PASSED
9 well-behaved-quic-server Compliant QUIC server handshake baseline server PASSED

QB — QUIC Transport Parameters & ALPN

QUIC INFO 2 scenarios

Corrupts QUIC transport parameters and ALPN negotiation. Tests invalid transport parameter values and ALPN/SNI fuzzing within QUIC Initial packets.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 quic-transport-params-corrupt QUIC Handshake packet with corrupted transport parameters client DROPPED
2 quic-alpn-sni-fuzz QUIC Initial with oversized ALPN in TLS extensions client DROPPED

QC — QUIC Resource Exhaustion & DoS

QUIC INFO 3 scenarios

QUIC resource exhaustion and DoS attacks. Tests CRYPTO frame buffer gaps, amplification padding attacks, and connection flood scenarios.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 quic-crypto-buffer-gaps QUIC CRYPTO frame with huge offset to test buffer gap handling client DROPPED
2 quic-dos-amplification-padding QUIC Initial with excessive padding to test amplification limits client DROPPED
3 quic-post-handshake-slowloris Completes QUIC handshake, then drip-feeds 1 byte per second to exhaust idle timeouts client TIMEOUT

QD — QUIC Flow Control & Stream Errors

QUIC INFO 3 scenarios

QUIC flow control and stream error fuzzing. Tests ACK range manipulation, stream data overlap, and invalid stream frame fields.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 quic-ack-range-fuzz QUIC ACK frame with invalid largest acknowledged and multiple blocks client DROPPED
2 quic-stream-overlap Multiple STREAM frames with overlapping offsets client DROPPED
3 quic-post-handshake-garbage Completes QUIC handshake, then floods the 1-RTT stream with 1MB of random binary noise client DROPPED

QE — QUIC Connection Migration & Path

QUIC INFO 1 scenario

QUIC connection migration and path validation fuzzing. Tests path challenge/response manipulation.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 quic-path-validation-fuzz Spamming PATH_CHALLENGE and PATH_RESPONSE frames client DROPPED

QF — QUIC Frame Structure & Mutation

QUIC INFO 2 scenarios

QUIC frame structure mutations. Sends undefined frame types and tests post-handshake HTTP request smuggling attempts.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 quic-undefined-frames QUIC packet containing undefined frame types (0x40-0xff) client DROPPED
2 quic-post-handshake-http-smuggling Completes QUIC handshake, then sends overlapping/malformed HTTP/1.1 syntax over the stream client DROPPED

QG — QUIC-TLS Handshake Order & State

QUIC INFO 711 scenarios

Maps TLS handshake order violation scenarios (Category A-G) into QUIC CRYPTO frames. Tests the same state machine attacks but delivered over QUIC Initial packets instead of raw TCP. Largest QUIC category with 711 scenarios.

Show all 711 scenarios
# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 quic-tls-out-of-order-finished-first-small-ch Send Finished before ClientHello [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
2 quic-tls-out-of-order-finished-first-pqc-ch Send Finished before ClientHello [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
3 quic-tls-out-of-order-cke-before-hello-small-ch Send ClientKeyExchange before ClientHello [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
4 quic-tls-out-of-order-cke-before-hello-pqc-ch Send ClientKeyExchange before ClientHello [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
5 quic-tls-duplicate-client-hello-small-ch Send ClientHello twice [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
6 quic-tls-duplicate-client-hello-pqc-ch Send ClientHello twice [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
7 quic-tls-client-hello-after-finished-small-ch Send ClientHello, receive ServerHello, then send another ClientHello [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
8 quic-tls-client-hello-after-finished-pqc-ch Send ClientHello, receive ServerHello, then send another ClientHello [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED

11 quic-tls-ccs-then-plaintext-handshake-small-ch Send CCS (signaling cipher activated) then send Finished as plaintext [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
12 quic-tls-ccs-then-plaintext-handshake-pqc-ch Send CCS (signaling cipher activated) then send Finished as plaintext [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED

14 quic-tls-renegotiation-downgrade-version-pqc-ch ClientHello with TLS 1.2, then renegotiation ClientHello advertising only TLS 1.0 [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
15 quic-tls-renegotiation-downgrade-cipher-small-ch Initial ClientHello with strong ciphers, renegotiation ClientHello only offering weak/export ciphers [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED

18 quic-tls-renegotiation-drop-extensions-pqc-ch Initial ClientHello with all extensions, renegotiation strips renegotiation_info and security extensions [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED

20 quic-tls-supported-groups-change-retry-pqc-ch ClientHello lists X25519+P-256, retry ClientHello lists only FFDHE2048 [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED

QH — QUIC-TLS Parameter & Extension Fuzzing

QUIC INFO 142 scenarios

Maps TLS parameter mutation and extension fuzzing (Category C, H, M, R) into QUIC. Tests ClientHello field mutations, extension structure attacks, and parameter conflicts delivered via QUIC.

Show all 142 scenarios
# Scenario Name Description Side Expected

QI — QUIC-TLS Record & Alert Injection

QUIC INFO 108 scenarios

Maps TLS alert injection and record layer attacks (Category D, F, S, T) into QUIC. Tests alert message injection and record format violations within QUIC CRYPTO frames.

QJ — QUIC-TLS Known CVEs & PQC

QUIC INFO 234 scenarios

Maps TLS CVE detection and PQC scenarios (Category I, J) plus cipher/signature fuzzing (Category V) into QUIC. Probes for Heartbleed, POODLE, and other CVEs over QUIC transport.

QK — QUIC-TLS Certificate Fuzzing

QUIC INFO 24 scenarios

Maps TLS client certificate abuse scenarios (Category X) into QUIC. Tests unsolicited certificates, malformed cert chains, and validation bypass attempts over QUIC.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 quic-tls-client-cert-unsolicited-post-hello-small-ch After CH→SH exchange, client sends Certificate without CertificateRequest [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
2 quic-tls-client-cert-unsolicited-post-hello-pqc-ch After CH→SH exchange, client sends Certificate without CertificateRequest [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
3 quic-tls-client-cert-before-hello-small-ch Client sends Certificate BEFORE ClientHello [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
4 quic-tls-client-cert-before-hello-pqc-ch Client sends Certificate BEFORE ClientHello [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
5 quic-tls-client-cert-double-small-ch Client sends two Certificate messages back-to-back [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
6 quic-tls-client-cert-double-pqc-ch Client sends two Certificate messages back-to-back [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
7 quic-tls-client-cert-empty-chain-small-ch Client sends Certificate message with 0 certificates in chain [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
8 quic-tls-client-cert-empty-chain-pqc-ch Client sends Certificate message with 0 certificates in chain [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
9 quic-tls-client-cert-garbage-der-small-ch Client sends Certificate with random garbage as DER cert data [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
10 quic-tls-client-cert-garbage-der-pqc-ch Client sends Certificate with random garbage as DER cert data [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
11 quic-tls-client-cert-oversized-small-ch Client sends Certificate with 32KB of cert data [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
12 quic-tls-client-cert-oversized-pqc-ch Client sends Certificate with 32KB of cert data [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
13 quic-tls-client-cert-verify-without-cert-small-ch Client sends CertificateVerify without prior Certificate message [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
14 quic-tls-client-cert-verify-without-cert-pqc-ch Client sends CertificateVerify without prior Certificate message [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
15 quic-tls-client-cert-verify-bad-signature-small-ch Client sends Certificate + CertificateVerify with random (invalid) signature [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
16 quic-tls-client-cert-verify-bad-signature-pqc-ch Client sends Certificate + CertificateVerify with random (invalid) signature [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
17 quic-tls-client-cert-verify-wrong-algorithm-small-ch CertificateVerify with undefined signature algorithm 0xFFFF [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
18 quic-tls-client-cert-verify-wrong-algorithm-pqc-ch CertificateVerify with undefined signature algorithm 0xFFFF [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
19 quic-tls-client-cert-cn-mismatch-small-ch Client certificate with CN completely unrelated to server hostname [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
20 quic-tls-client-cert-cn-mismatch-pqc-ch Client certificate with CN completely unrelated to server hostname [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
21 quic-tls-client-cert-self-signed-ca-small-ch Client certificate claiming to be CA with basicConstraints cA=TRUE [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
22 quic-tls-client-cert-self-signed-ca-pqc-ch Client certificate claiming to be CA with basicConstraints cA=TRUE [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
23 quic-tls-client-cert-and-verify-before-hello-small-ch Certificate + CertificateVerify both sent before ClientHello [small CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED
24 quic-tls-client-cert-and-verify-before-hello-pqc-ch Certificate + CertificateVerify both sent before ClientHello [PQC big CH] [via QUIC Initial] client DROPPED

QS — QUIC Server-Side Fuzzing

QUIC HIGH 17 scenarios

QUIC server-side fuzzing — the fuzzer acts as a malicious QUIC server. Sends stream resets, STOP_SENDING frames, invalid transport parameters, and malformed server responses.

# Scenario Name Description Side Expected
1 quic-stream-reset RESET_STREAM frame with 0xdeadbeef error code targeting a random stream server DROPPED
2 quic-stop-sending STOP_SENDING frame with garbage error code to abort stream mid-transfer server DROPPED
3 quic-connection-close CONNECTION_CLOSE with corrupted UTF-8 in reason phrase server DROPPED
4 quic-flow-control MAX_DATA and MAX_STREAM_DATA frames with zero-window to exhaust flow control server DROPPED
5 quic-cid-migration PATH_CHALLENGE frame to trigger CID migration probing server DROPPED
6 quic-middlebox-evasion GREASE version number in long header to probe middlebox and firewall behavior server DROPPED
7 quic-random-payload Short-header packet with entirely random payload bytes server DROPPED
8 quic-server-retry-flood Flood client with 50 Retry packets to overwhelm retry logic server DROPPED
9 quic-server-version-negotiation-invalid Version Negotiation listing only invalid/unknown versions server DROPPED
10 quic-server-initial-flood Flood client with Initial packets containing garbage ServerHello server DROPPED
11 quic-server-handshake-invalid-cert Handshake packet with corrupt certificate data in CRYPTO frame server DROPPED
12 quic-server-connection-close-abuse Rapid CONNECTION_CLOSE frames with misleading error codes server DROPPED
13 quic-server-stateless-reset-flood Flood with packets resembling Stateless Reset tokens server DROPPED
14 quic-server-malformed-transport-params Initial response with corrupt transport parameters in CRYPTO frame server DROPPED
15 quic-server-amplification-exploit Response exceeding 3x client Initial size (violates anti-amplification) server DROPPED
16 quic-server-zero-length-cid Response packets with zero-length connection IDs server DROPPED
17 quic-server-path-challenge-flood Flood PATH_CHALLENGE frames to exhaust client resources server DROPPED

QSCAN — QUIC Compatibility Scanning (Non-fuzzing)

QUIC INFO 46 scenarios

Non-fuzzing QUIC compatibility scanning. Probes QUIC version 1 and version 2 with different cipher suites, named groups (including PQC), and ALPN values to map server support.

Show all 46 scenarios
